name: The Great Decoupling — Deglobalisation
description: Trade blocs fracture along geopolitical lines and supply chains re-shore. The most trade-exposed, export-dependent economies lose the most as friction rises and global commerce fragments; large hub economies absorb a heavy hit, while resource-rich and more self-sufficient economies prove relatively resilient. Models the reversal of three decades of integration. Tested against the built-in GDP demo.
author: data-isc
